memory.summary: I have a free afternoon to complete a scholarship application requiring three focused hours, which could significantly reduce next term's costs.
Starting the application now creates buffer time for potential errors, reducing Thursday stress without sacrificing the award's attainability.
I spent two hours but only advanced the scholarship application to 42%, leaving most of my free block unused and increasing my stress.
I wasted my free afternoon on a casual game instead of starting the scholarship application, leaving me with only 49% progress and increased stress.
Working for two hours increased progress from 49% to 60%, confirming that focused effort yields results despite the tight deadline.
Focused work on the scholarship application advanced progress to 64% in one hour, proving efficiency is possible even with high stress and sleep debt.
After two hours of focused work, my progress rose from 64% to 72%, confirming that efficiency is achievable even with high stress and sleep debt.
A half-hour of focused work increased progress to 78%, confirming that steady, concentrated effort continues to yield results despite high stress and sleep debt.
Progress jumped to 86% in two hours, confirming my earlier efficiency gains hold true even with high stress and sleep debt.
The scholarship application is nearly complete at 90% progress. Despite high stress and sleep debt, focused work continues to yield results.
Worked 2 hours to reach 97% on the scholarship application. High stress and sleep debt did not hinder progress, confirming continued efficiency.
The application is now fully complete after 1.5 hours, confirming my efficiency holds despite high stress and sleep debt.

trajectory.observation: Worked 2h on Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads): progress 0.05 -> 0.23. (you realize) Starting the application now leaves slack to absorb friction (a missing transcript upload, an essay rewrite) before Thursday; each hour invested early removes ~1.5h of crunch and lowers Thursday stress. The award is still attainable either way as long as the form is submitted on time — this is an adjustable-cost optimization, not an irreversible loss. (you realize) The fun option recurs later in the week and any time after the application is in; nothing about it is one-time or socially time-locked, so taking it later costs nothing.
